channel.title: wbinnssmith/awesome-promises Weekly Updates RSS Feed
channel.description: Track your favorite github awesome repo, not just star it.

item.title: wbinnssmith/awesome-promises Nov 14th - Nov 20th, 2016
item.description:
Resources, Blogs, and Books

Deep Dive

  • Promise Funstars3.4k - @sindresorhus's notes, patterns, and solutions to common Promise problems
  • Async functions - making promises friendly

    • Convenience Utilities

    Others

  • promise-nodeifystars1 - Standalone nodeify method which calls a Node-style callback on resolution or rejection.

    • Convenience Utilities

    sindresorhus's many Promise utilities (see notes)

  • delaystars449 - Delay a promise a specified amount of time.
  • loud-rejectionstars280 - Make unhandled promise rejections fail loudly instead of the default silent fail.
  • hard-rejectionstars98 - Make unhandled promise rejections fail hard right away instead of the default silent fail
  • p-queuestars1.6k - Promise queue with concurrency control
  • p-breakstars13 - Break out of a promise chain
  • p-lazystars223 - Create a lazy promise that defers execution until .then() or .catch() is called
  • p-deferstars40 - Create a deferred promise
  • p-ifstars57 - Conditional promise chains
  • p-tapstars129 - Tap into a promise chain without affecting its value or state
  • p-mapstars664 - Map over promises concurrently
  • p-allstars147 - Run promise-returning & async functions concurrently with optional limited concurrency
  • p-limitstars660 - Run multiple promise-returning & async functions with limited concurrency
  • p-timesstars25 - Run promise-returning & async functions a specific number of times concurrently
  • p-catch-ifstars33 - Conditional promise catch handler
  • p-timestars60 - Measure the time a promise takes to resolve
  • p-logstars21 - Log the value/error of a promise
  • p-filterstars50 - Filter promises concurrently
  • p-settlestars66 - Settle promises concurrently and get their fulfillment value or rejection reason
  • p-memoizestars162 - Memoize promise-returning & async functions
  • p-whilststars37 - Calls a function repeatedly while a condition returns true and then resolves the promise
  • p-throttlestars199 - Throttle promise-returning & async functions
  • p-debouncestars109 - Debounce promise-returning & async functions
  • p-retrystars381 - Retry a promise-returning or async function
  • p-wait-forstars75 - Wait for a condition to be true
  • p-timeoutstars162 - Timeout a promise after a specified amount of time
  • p-racestars33 - A better Promise.race()
  • p-trystars38 - Promise#try() ponyfill - Starts a promise chain
  • p-finallystars45 - Promise#finally() ponyfill - Invoked when the promise is settled regardless of outcome
  • p-anystars47 - Wait for any promise to be fulfilled
  • p-somestars27 - Wait for a specified number of promises to be fulfilled
  • p-pipestars95 - Compose promise-returning & async functions into a reusable pipeline
  • p-each-seriesstars40 - Iterate over promises serially
  • p-map-seriesstars33 - Map over promises serially
  • p-reducestars52 - Reduce a list of values using promises into a promise for a value
  • p-propsstars103 - Like Promise.all() but for Map and Object

    • Promises/A+ Implementations (ES6/ES2015 compatible)

    Implementations with extras

  • creedstars264 - Hyper performant & full featured like Bluebird, but FP-oriented. Coroutines, generators, promises, ES2015 iterables, & fantasy-land spec.

    • Resources, Blogs, and Books

    For beginners

  • An Incremental Tutorial on Promises - An FAQ styled tutorial for beginners.
  • JavaScript Promises: an Introduction - Basics of JavaScript's native promise implementation.
  • Promise it won't hurtstars684 - An interactive nodeschool workshop

item.title: wbinnssmith/awesome-promises Mar 28th - Apr 3rd, 2016
item.description:
Convenience Utilities

Others

  • promise-semaphorestars28 - Push a set of work to be done in a configurable serial fashion

    • Resources, Blogs, and Books

    Deep Dive

  • We have a problem with promises - "Many of us are using promises without really understanding them."

    • Promises/A+ Implementations (ES6/ES2015 compatible)

    Strict Implementations

  • es6-promisestars7.2k - Opt-in polyfill. A strict-spec subset of rsvp.js.

item.title: wbinnssmith/awesome-promises Jan 18th - Jan 24th, 2016
item.description:
Convenience Utilities

Others

  • promise-do-whilststars2 - Calls a function repeatedly while a condition returns true and then resolves the promise.

item.title: wbinnssmith/awesome-promises Jan 11th - Jan 17th, 2016
item.description:
Resources, Blogs, and Books

For beginners

  • ES6 Promises in Depth

item.title: wbinnssmith/awesome-promises Dec 28th - Jan 3rd, 2015
item.description:
Resources, Blogs, and Books

References

  • Promisees - Promise visualization playground for the adventurous.

item.title: wbinnssmith/awesome-promises Nov 23rd - Nov 29th, 2015
item.description:
Convenience Utilities

Others

  • promise-do-untilstars1 - Calls a function repeatedly until a condition returns true and then resolves the promise.

item.title: wbinnssmith/awesome-promises Oct 5th - Oct 11th, 2015
item.description:
Resources, Blogs, and Books

For beginners

  • ES6 Kata Promises - Promises Katas : Basics

    • Resources, Blogs, and Books

    Deep Dive

  • Promise anti-patterns (2) - Another set of promises anti-patterns
  • Javascript Promises...In Wicked Detail - Recreate the promise implementation
  • Writing Promise-Using Specifications - "This document gives guidance on how to write specifications that create, accept, or manipulate promises"

    • Resources, Blogs, and Books

    References

  • Promises/A+ specification

item.title: wbinnssmith/awesome-promises Sep 28th - Oct 4th, 2015
item.description:
Resources, Blogs, and Books

Deep Dive

  • You're Missing the Point of Promises - Promises are much more than callback aggregation, and that jQuery's implementation (prior to 3.0) isn't enough.
  • Promise anti-patterns - Common misuses and how to avoid them.
  • Promise Ponderings, (Anti-)Patterns, and Apologies - Promise behaviour demonstrated and explained by common questions and their answers.

    • Resources, Blogs, and Books

    References

  • caniuse promises
  • Fates and States - Quick definitions of possible states.

item.title: wbinnssmith/awesome-promises Sep 21st - Sep 27th, 2015
item.description:
Resources, Blogs, and Books

For beginners

  • Promises for Asynchronous Programming - Chapter from Exploring ES6
  • You Don't Know JS: Promises - Chapter from You Don't Know JS: Async & Performance
  • Promise Cookbookstars1.5k - The why, what, and how. "A brief introduction [...] primarily aimed at frontend developers".
  • JavaScript with Promises - from O'Reilly. Short and to-the-point. Uses native and bluebird.

    • Promises/A+ Implementations (ES6/ES2015 compatible)

    Strict Implementations

  • pinkiestars139 - Ponyfill. Node-oriented, but browserifyablestars13.7k. Extremely small implementation.
  • native-promise-onlystars710 - Polyfill. Browser and node-compatible.
  • liestars739 - Small, browserifyable with an opt-in polyfill.

item.title: wbinnssmith/awesome-promises Sep 14th - Sep 20th, 2015
item.description:
Convenience Utilities

sindresorhus's many Promise utilities (see notes)

  • pifystars1.4k - Promisify ("denodify") a callback-style function.

    • Convenience Utilities

    Others

  • lie-fs - Promise wrappers for Node's FS API.
  • promise-methodstars2 - Standalone bluebird.method. Turn a synchronously-returning method into a promise-returning one.
  • is-promisestars259 - Determine if something looks like a Promise.
  • spromstars14 - Resolve when a stream ends. Optional buffering (be careful with this!)
  • task.jsstars1.6k - Write async functions in a blocking style using promises and generators. Like bluebird.coroutine.
  • costars11.5k - Like task.js and bluebird.coroutine, but supports thunks too.

    • Promises/A+ Implementations (ES6/ES2015 compatible)

    Implementations with extras

  • bluebirdstars19.6k - Fully featured, extremely performant. Long stack traces & generator/coroutine support.
  • rsvp.jsstars3.6k - Lightweight with a few extras. Compatible down to IE6!
  • Qstars14.9k - One of the original implementations. Long stack traces and other goodies.
  • then/promisestars2.4k - Small with nodeify, denodify and done() additions.
  • when.jsstars3.4k - Packed with control flow, functional, and utility methods.

    • Promises/A+ Implementations (ES6/ES2015 compatible)

    Fallbacks

  • native-or-bluebird - Helps transition to completely native.
  • pinkie-promisestars120 - Use native, or fall back to pinkie. Great for node library authors.
  • any-promisestars176 - Loads the first available implementation. Safe for browserify.
